architecture.summary: Redesign from sequential single-stream to parallel per-case SSE with Semaphore concurrency control (default 3). Server: extract runSingleCaseSSE, new per-case endpoints, Promise.allSettled for bulk. Client: useMultiSSE hook with per-case AbortControllers, CaseRunState map replacing global isRunning.

integrations.summary: N/A - internal refactor of existing eval-server and eval-ui. No new external integrations. LLM calls via existing LlmClient abstraction.

ui-ux.summary: Per-case Run/AB/Cancel buttons replacing global controls. Independent case states (idle/queued/running/complete/error/cancelled). Cases can run, complete, and show results independently while others are still running.

performance.summary: Semaphore with default concurrency of 3 to limit parallel LLM calls. Promise.allSettled for bulk runs. This is a local dev tool so traffic/CDN/SLA not applicable.

security.summary: N/A - local dev tool (eval-server). No auth, no PII, no public endpoints. Same security posture as existing benchmark system.

edge-cases.summary: Per-case cancellation mid-stream. Semaphore release on error/cancel. Bulk cancel must abort all in-flight cases. Race condition: cancel during queued-to-running transition. History writes for partial bulk runs (only completed cases). SSE stream cleanup on client disconnect.
